channel.title: Daily Briefing
channel.description: Your AI-powered daily news podcast covering technology, AI, world affairs, and business & finance.
channel.itunes:author: Daily Briefing Podcast

item.title: Daily Briefing — Monday, April 13, 2026
item.description:
Daily Briefing — Monday, April 13, 2026
Good morning, everyone. It’s Monday, April 13th, 2026, and welcome to the Daily Briefing. I'm Marcus, and we're here to bring you up-to-date on today's key stories across technology, artificial intelligence, world affairs, and business. We're starting with a significant change from the gaming platform Roblox, which is tightening age verification processes for young users. That's right, Marcus. I'm Sofia, and we'll also dive into some intriguing developments in AI today, examining how businesses are adapting to new coding paradigms. Plus, there’s big news on the world affairs front, including the U.S.'s decision to blockade Iranian ports following failed peace talks. Indeed, Sofia. And in the world of business and finance, we'll explore how recent geopolitical moves might be affecting market dynamics, particularly with commodities and treasury yields reacting to the situation in Iran. Let’s get started with our technology…

item.title: Daily Briefing — Sunday, April 12, 2026
item.description:
Daily Briefing — Sunday, April 12, 2026
Good morning, it's Sunday, April 12, 2026. Welcome to another episode of "Daily Briefing". I'm Marcus. And I'm Sofia. Today, we've got a jam-packed show for you with the latest updates from the world of technology, artificial intelligence, world affairs, and finance. That's right, Sofia. Starting with the technology segment, there's a significant ripple effect in the energy market as ongoing geopolitical tensions from the Iran war have cast a shadow over the US's energy dominance strategy. It's an important issue, Marcus. The situation is affecting global oil prices, and we'll dig into how that's impacting consumers at the pump later in the segment. In artificial intelligence, we're hearing interesting debates about the implications of AI development not hitting a wall anytime soon. According to MIT Technology Review, this could signal new frontiers in technological capabilities. Absolutely, and there are…

item.title: Daily Briefing — Friday, April 10, 2026
item.description:
Daily Briefing — Friday, April 10, 2026
Good morning, everyone! It’s Friday, April 10, 2026, and you're listening to the "Daily Briefing." I'm Marcus, your anchor today. We've got a packed show ahead, so let's get right into it. In our technology segment, we'll dive into an exciting partnership between Snap and Qualcomm that’s setting the stage for their new AR glasses. And I’m Sofia. Absolutely, Marcus. It seems like Snap is finally moving forward after a bit of a standstill, and that Qualcomm partnership is definitely something to pay attention to. Beyond the tech realm, artificial intelligence continues to make headlines. Today, we'll discuss OpenAI's introduction of a new ChatGPT Pro tier at a hefty price tag, but with increased usage limits. However, some claims around their capabilities need more scrutiny. That’s right, Marcus. We'll also delve into some interesting, albeit single-sourced, insights about Anthropic's Mythos,…

item.title: Daily Briefing — Saturday, April 11, 2026
item.description:
Daily Briefing — Saturday, April 11, 2026
Welcome to Daily Briefing. It’s Saturday, April 11, 2026, and I’m Marcus, here with your essential guide to the news. And I’m Sofia. We're diving into tech, global affairs, the latest market movements, and much more today. Marcus, start us off with a peek into the tech world. Absolutely, Sofia. Top of the list, we’ve got an interesting error from Google. Polymarket bets, which are basically predictions on market events, appeared on Google News inadvertently. Not exactly what you'd expect to see mixed in with your regular headlines. Quite the algorithm hiccup, Marcus. And sticking with the digital realm, we've got some launch issues with Pokémon Champions. Bugs are impacting gameplay, and fans aren't thrilled. It's the latest in a series of rocky game releases. Over in AI, there's big talk about improving security measures. According to VentureBeat, experts are…

item.title: Daily Briefing — Thursday, April 9, 2026
item.description:
Daily Briefing — Thursday, April 9, 2026
**INTRO** Good morning, everyone. It's Thursday, April 9, 2026, and you're listening to "Daily Briefing." I'm Marcus. And I'm Sofia. We've got some fascinating stories for you today. Marcus, why don't you start us off with what's happening in technology? Sure thing, Sofia. Starting with tech, Amazon is gearing up to launch its satellite internet service, Leo, in mid-2026. It's a big move that's set to rival SpaceX's Starlink. Also, there are new updates on the much-talked-about MacBook Neo. Absolutely, Marcus. And then there's Spotify, making headlines by allowing users to turn off all videos. Plus, Google is stirring up quite the conversation with an AI-powered deepfake tool. Exciting stuff! Now, in the world of artificial intelligence, we're diving into the rapid advancements of AI models and a new framework allowing AI to rewrite their own skills without retraining. That’s…

item.title: Daily Briefing — Wednesday, April 8, 2026
item.description:
Daily Briefing — Wednesday, April 8, 2026
Welcome to "Daily Briefing," your trusted source for news that matters, delivered straight to your ears. Today is Wednesday, April 8th, 2026. I’m Marcus, and alongside me is Sofia, ready to dive deep into today’s top stories. First, we'll be exploring a promising development in tech with the launch of a unique USB-C AA battery — stay tuned to hear more about why that’s exciting for gadget lovers. Absolutely, Marcus. And moving into the world of AI, we have some intriguing updates, like Block's new AI agent, Managerbot. It’s poised to revolutionize how small businesses use artificial intelligence. Definitely one to watch. In world affairs, the U.S. and Iran have agreed to a two-week ceasefire, a development that has already begun affecting global markets. It’s a story rich with international implications. And speaking of markets, we'll see how the ceasefire…

item.title: Daily Briefing — Tuesday, April 7, 2026
item.description:
Daily Briefing — Tuesday, April 7, 2026
**INTRO** Hello and welcome to Daily Briefing! It’s Tuesday, April 7, 2026. I’m Marcus. And I’m Sofia. Thanks for joining us today. We have a lot to cover, starting with technology, where we're getting a sneak peek at a foldable iPhone and some intriguing moves in the AI sector. Absolutely fascinating, Marcus. And speaking of AI, we’ve got developments that might change how small businesses operate and potentially, the future of jobs as we know them. On the world stage, tensions are mounting with a looming deadline related to Iran that President Trump has set — it's shaping up to be a pivotal moment. And in the business world, big money moves with Universal Music and potential price hikes rippling from the conflict in the Middle East. Stick around as we unpack these stories and more. Let's dive in! **TECHNOLOGY…

item.title: Daily Briefing — Monday, April 6, 2026
item.description:
Daily Briefing — Monday, April 6, 2026
**INTRO** Good morning, everyone. It's Monday, April 6th, 2026, and welcome to the Daily Briefing. I'm Marcus, here with the key stories you need to know today. Our top story in technology is about potential AI influences on search engine results — a development that might change how we see the digital landscape. That's right, Marcus. And in the world of artificial intelligence, we're looking at how small online sellers are leveraging AI to guide their production decisions. It's an interesting intersection of tech and commerce. In world affairs, tensions are high as Iran and the United States trade threats over the Strait of Hormuz, which has significant implications for global energy supplies. Quite important, Marcus, and in the business and finance arena, we're focusing on Jamie Dimon's latest shareholder letter, where he discusses major risks in geopolitics and AI.
